services.title: What I
services.description: Deep expertise in civil law, built on {{years}} years of practice and hundreds of successful cases.
services.cta_desc: Describe your issue — we will help.

divorce.name: Marriage & divorce
divorce.desc: Professional support throughout the divorce procedure. Assistance in resolving disputes through court and out-of-court proceedings.
divorce.content:
A divorce attorney specialises in civil law. This field can be emotionally charged and full of life-changing decisions. A family law practitioner must handle a wide range of issues delicately yet fairly: from divorce and annulment to child custody, alimony and visitation rights.

The divorce process can take from three to twelve months, depending on how the documents are filed and the number of disputed issues involved.
details: Advice on divorce procedures
Preparation of divorce petitions
Court representation without the client's attendance
Recognition of marriage as invalid
Establishing a separate residence regime

alimony.name: Alimony
alimony.desc: Protecting the child's rights and securing their financial well-being. Recovery and review of payment amounts.
alimony.content:
Alimony will be one of the most discussed topics in your divorce negotiations. Also known as spousal support, alimony is money paid by one former spouse to the other after a divorce.

The goal is for both spouses, regardless of income level, to maintain a standard of living after divorce similar to that during the marriage. It also helps the spouse who raised children and managed the household to avoid being left without means of support.

There are various types of spousal support arrangements for a divorcing couple to consider. Consult a qualified attorney to find out which option is best for you.
details: Recovery of alimony as a fixed sum or a share of income
Recovery of additional child expenses
Reduction or increase of alimony amount
Recovery of spousal maintenance
Holding the payer liable for non-payment

property.name: Property division
property.desc: Fair distribution of jointly acquired marital property, protection of ownership rights to housing and business.
property.content:
When a marriage breaks down, a wide range of legal decisions must be made regarding the couple's assets — determining what must be divided and what constitutes marital property. This can be a confusing and complex process.

When this happens — or, ideally, before it does — you should consult an experienced family law attorney.

Make sure your needs and rights are properly represented in court from the outset, and seek the help of an experienced attorney who will work closely with you to ensure your case is handled properly.
details: Division of real estate (apartments, houses, land)
Division of vehicles and joint businesses
Disputes over division of loans and debts
Recognition of property as personal private ownership
Conclusion of property division agreements

children.name: Custody & children
children.desc: Resolution of the most complex issues relating to the upbringing and residence of children, with their best interests in mind.
children.content:
Struggling to prove that you are the best parent in the eyes of the court? Understanding the 'best interests of the child' standard and what courts are really looking for will help you win custody and relieve the stress of your current situation.

Parents hoping to win custody of their children should first familiarise themselves with the custody laws in their jurisdiction and be prepared to present themselves in the best light in court. It is also important to understand that the sole goal of the court is the best interests of the child, which may or may not include sole custody.
details: Determination of the child's place of residence
Establishing a schedule for communication and participation in upbringing
Termination and restoration of parental rights
Obtaining permission for the child to travel abroad
Removing obstacles to communication with the child

inheritance.name: Inheritance
inheritance.desc: Legal assistance with inheritance processing and resolution of inheritance disputes through the courts.
inheritance.content:
If you have been wrongfully excluded from a will, or if your lawful inheritance is under threat due to the actions of others, you have the right to protection. It is important to act quickly, as there are strict deadlines that must not be missed when protecting or recovering an inheritance.

The first step is to contact me for an assessment of your situation. An experienced inheritance attorney will help protect your property rights.
details: Processing inheritance by law and by will
Renewal of the deadline for accepting an inheritance
Recognition of ownership rights through inheritance
Contesting wills in court
Determination of an additional deadline for submitting an inheritance acceptance application

domestic_violence.name: Domestic violence
domestic_violence.desc: Urgent legal protection for domestic violence victims. Obtaining restraining orders.
domestic_violence.content: The law on combating domestic violence focuses on protection from violence within the family and between partners. It provides criminal and administrative penalties for those who cause emotional or physical harm to persons with whom they share a family or other close relationship.
details: Obtaining a restraining order against the abuser
Defence of interests in administrative offence proceedings
Representation with the police and law enforcement agencies
Legal support throughout the prosecution process

housing.name: Housing disputes
housing.desc: Protection of housing rights, resolution of conflicts regarding registration and eviction.
details: Eviction from or moving into residential premises
Recognition of a person as having lost the right to use housing
Disputes over the use of residential premises
Removal of obstacles to the use of property

adoption.name: Adoption
adoption.desc: Full support throughout the adoption procedure. Assistance with document preparation for guardianship authorities.
details: Legal consultations on adoption matters
Preparation of a document package for submission to court
Representation before guardianship and custody authorities
Adoption of a child by a stepparent

property_rights.name: Property rights
property_rights.desc: Protection and registration of ownership rights to real estate and other valuable assets.
property_rights.content:
The legal definition of property is 'everything belonging to a natural or legal person'. Property can be a tangible or intangible object or, in some cases, an idea owned by someone. Ownership rights give the owner the right to possess and use property, as well as the right to exclude any other rights to possess and use that property.

Property rights and personal freedom are interrelated. The mission of a property rights attorney is to steadfastly protect clients' property rights against state and private abuses.
details: Protection of property rights through the courts
Recognition of ownership of new buildings and unauthorised construction
Preparation of real estate documents
Support of real estate transactions

about.title: An attorney you can trust
about.p1: I am Daria Oleksandrivna Bohdashkina, an attorney with {{years}} years of experience in civil law. My approach is based not only on legislation, but also on empathy.
about.p2: I understand that civil disputes are always an emotional ordeal. Every client receives my personal attention and an honest assessment of their situation.
about.p3: I do not aim to take on hundreds of cases simultaneously. My philosophy is deep immersion in each individual story to find the solution that best protects your interests.

items.q: How much does the first consultation cost?
items.a: The first consultation is free. During it we get acquainted, I analyse your situation and propose a defence strategy. No obligations after the first conversation.

items.q: How quickly can a meeting be arranged?
items.a: Usually a meeting (in person or online) can be arranged within 24 hours of your enquiry. In urgent situations — within a few hours.

items.q: Do I need to attend the hearings?
items.a: In most cases (divorce, property division) I can represent your interests entirely on my own. Your attendance is not required if we arrange the appropriate power of attorney.

items.q: How long does the divorce process take?
items.a: Through the civil registry office — 1 month. Through court — from 2 to 6 months, depending on disputes over children or property. I always strive to speed up the process as much as possible.
